about:
Nira Pollock MD PhD is the Co-Director of the Infectious Diseases Diagnostic Laboratory at Boston Children’s Hospital and a faculty member of the Division of Infectious Diseases at Beth Israel Deaconess Medical Center (BIDMC) in Boston. Since 2023 she has also worked part-time (under an IPA agreement) for the National Institute of Biomedical Imaging and Bioengineering/NIH as a Senior Scientific Advisor for Diagnostic Development Strategy providing guidance to RADx/ITAP programs for diagnostic development and assisting with interagency pandemic preparedness efforts.

Dr. Pollock is an Associate Professor of both Medicine and Pathology at Harvard Medical School. She completed her MD/PhD at the University of California San Francisco her medical residency at Brigham and Women’s Hospital in Boston and her infectious diseases/clinical microbiology fellowships at BIDMC.
 
Dr. Pollock is an expert in the development and evaluation of novel diagnostics for infectious diseases with experience ranging from early proof of principle laboratory demonstration through translation to field implementation. Her diagnostics research has spanned a range of diseases including active and latent tuberculosis C. difficile infection influenza malaria Lyme disease STDs Ebola virus disease (EVD) HCV and COVID-19. Her research has also involved a wide range of technologies from simple paper-based lateral flow and microfluidic platforms to novel automated platforms for protein and nucleic acid detection and a wide range of testing scenarios from the high-complexity molecular laboratory to point-of-care testing (including in resource-limited and outbreak settings). 
Dr. Pollock is a member of the American Society of Clinical Investigation and a fellow of the Infectious Diseases Society of America (IDSA).

Built on a solid multidisciplinary foundation of humanities social sciences natural sciences medical sciences and engineering SYSU is propelled forward by the continuous pursuit of academic innovation. Equipped with a globally aware outlook the University has dedicated itself to being an institution that is "Comprehensive Innovative and Open". company_description:
At Children's Hospital Colorado we know that kids aren't just mini adults. They're in a chapter of life unlike any other and deserve incredibly different care to match. That's what drives our distinct thoughtful approach to pediatric healthcare. It's not just about the treatments we offer but how we offer them.
 
Our work has earned us a spot among the best children's hospitals in the nation and we proudly provide exceptional care to kids and families across Colorado and the region. Our goal is to ensure that every child receives personalized care that blends deep expertise and experience with compassion and a special understanding of what kids need to heal.
 
Our commitment to children fuels us but our team members make our achievements possible. Without a culture that prioritizes the beauty of difference and the importance of a thriving workforce we cannot provide the kind of care we strive for. By allowing each team member to explore their passions find flexibility outside the office and succeed through collaboration we continue to reach new heights in pediatric health.
 
When you work at Children's Colorado you're joining thousands of team members representing the full spectrum of pediatric specialties and professional roles. Our system of care includes four pediatric hospitals 10+ specialty care centers and 1 300+ outreach clinics. about:
Dr. Gonzalez is a Board Certified fellowship-trained Orthopaedic Foot and Ankle Surgeon. He earned his Bachelor of Science from University of California Berkeley MBA from UCLA Anderson School of Management and medical degree from UCLA David Geffen School of Medicine. Dr. Gonzalez completed his residency training in orthopaedic surgery at Harvard Medical School then went on to pursue a subspecialty fellowship training in orthopaedic foot and ankle surgery at Cedar Sinai Medical Center in Los Angeles. 

Throughout his career Dr. Gonzalez has focused on improving patient care medical education entrepreneurship and research. His committed interest and passion in translational clinical and biomechanical research has prompted over 75 peer-reviewed scientific publications and presentations as well as several book chapters in orthopedic surgery. Additionally Dr. Gonzalez routinely lectures in regional national and international forums on foot and ankle topics and improving value-based patient care. As such Dr. Gonzalez acts as an active reviewer for both Foot and Ankle International Foot and Ankle Orthopaedics and Foot and Ankle Specialist. He is a member of various foot and ankle surgery societies including the AAOS the American Orthopaedic Foot and Ankle Society (AOFAS) AOFAS Post Graduate Education Committee and the AOFAS Physicians Resource Center(PRC) Committee.He is also the President and Co-Founder of Orthopaedic Foot and Ankle Society Inc. a collaboration of foot and ankle surgeons to improve delivery of health care innovate on current surgical technologies and advance overall patient care.

Dr. Gonzalez is a healthcare entrepreneur with experience in biotech and health tech startups. He was COO and head of Research Development for the biotech start up Phase Scientific form 2010-2012 and helped build their platform for development and large commercialization.  

Currently Dr. Gonzalez has changed his focus to the healthcare sector to improve efficiency and delivery of care. He has Co- founded and is CEO of GaleAI a coding and billing company using AI and NLP and CEO of Redwood Tech LLC a healthcare driven Think Tank to optimize and improve healthcare delivery technology and education.

Outside of these clinical interests Dr. Gonzalez's passions include medical education entrepreneurship healthcare management and policy inventing anything and everything patient quality and safety value based healthcare research and clinical outcomes research.
current_position_2_description: Dr. Gonzalez is a Clinical Assistant Professor of Orthopaedic Surgery at University of South Carolina who specializes in treating disorders of the foot and ankle. Here Dr. Gonzalez is a surgeon researcher and medical educator for both the medical students and orthopaedic surgery residents. Dr. Gonzalez has a clinical interest in foot and ankle reconstruction minimally invasive surgery ankle arthroscopy foot and ankle arthritis ankle replacement surgery deformity correction Charcot Marie Tooth disease sports-related injuries cartilage preservation and replacement surgery bunion and hammertoe surgery ligament/tendon disorders Dance medicine and orthopaedic trauma and fracture care.

company_description:
Quantum Materials Corp (QMC) is a world leader in the design and manufacturing of high-performance nanomaterials. QMC primarily focuses on luminescent semiconductor nanocrystals known as quantum dots and other nanoparticles for the electronics display lighting and photovoltaic industries. The Company has developed an industry-first continuous-flow manufacturing processes which unlike traditional batch processing delivers highly uniform products and is quickly and easily scalable. QMC has two metric tons of production capacity the largest of any manufacturer of cadmium-free quantum dots. 

QDX™ Quantum Dots are the latest nanotech breakthrough from Quantum Materials. Designed to withstand high heat oxidation and moisture without degrading QDX™ surmount implementation constraints due to lack of environmental stability. For example QDX™ will enable QD use in displays without expensive barrier films for encapsulation. In QD-LED lighting QDX™ will eventually enable on-chip application increasing energy efficiency reducing the need for bulky expensive heat sinks for the solid-state lighting market QDX™ will also lead the way in developing extremely low cost next-gen roll-to-roll printed QD solar cells. 

QMC's volume manufacturing methods enable consistent quality and scalable cost reductions to drive innovative discovery to commercial success. Quantum Materials'​ products are the foundation for technologically superior energy efficient and environmentally sound LCD UHD displays the next generation of solid-state lighting solar photovoltaic power applications advanced battery and energy storage solutions biotech imaging and biomedical theranostics. Wholly-owned subsidiary Solterra Renewable Technologies develops sustainable quantum dot solar technology. QMC produces both cadmium-based and RoHS compliant cadmium-free qd. Founded in 2007 Quantum Materials Corp. is based in San Marcos Texas and is traded on the OTCBB market under the symbol QTMM.

about: I obtained my Masters of Science in Biological Sciences from Virginia Tech. I worked in the laboratory of Dr. Joseph Falkinham and my research focused on emerging opportunistic premise pathogens Methylobacterium spp.. Opportunistic premise plumbing pathogens (OPPPs) are microbial residents of drinking water systems and premise plumbing that cause infection. Premise plumbing includes water pipes in hospitals houses apartment buildings or office buildings. OPPPs share a number of characteristics that contribute to their growth and survival in drinking water systems. Methylobacterium spp. strains were studied to measure growth in laboratory broth medium and drinking water measure hydrophobicity (ability to repel water) on surfaces found in household plumbing measure adherence to surfaces found in household plumbing and measure susceptibility to high temperatures. This study was able to determine which pipe surfaces support the least amount of Methylobacterium spp. growth to be used be used by plumbers and homeowners to reduce exposure to Methylobacterium spp.

For over 200 years the basic role of the United States Patent and Trademark Office (USPTO) has remained the same: to promote the progress of science and the useful arts by securing for limited times to inventors the exclusive right to their respective discoveries (Article 1 Section 8 of the United States Constitution). Under this system of protection American industry has flourished. New products have been invented new uses for old ones discovered and employment opportunities created for millions of Americans. The strength and vitality of the U.S. economy depends directly on effective mechanisms that protect new ideas and investments in innovation and creativity. The USPTO is at the cutting edge of the Nation's technological progress and achievement. 

The USPTO is a federal agency in the Department of Commerce. The USPTO occupies several interconnected buildings in Alexandria Virginia. company_description:
Exosome Diagnostics Inc. a subsidiary of mdxhealth is commercializing minimally invasive molecular diagnostics in blood and urine to enable doctors to select optimal therapies for cancer and other diseases. 
 
Exosomes are lipid bilayer covered microvesicles shed by solid tumors into body fluids such as blood and urine. Exosomes contain nucleic acid profiles of cancers and other diseases. In published studies Exosome Diagnostics has demonstrated the ability to extract exosomes from the blood and urine of patients and to detect with high sensitivity and specificity key genes and gene mutations associated with a variety of cancers.

Stochastic simulation of large-scale systems

company_description:
DNAe is commercializing its pioneering semiconductor sequencing technology for healthcare applications where rapid point-of-need diagnostics are of critical need including infectious disease and cancer testing and monitoring. It is developing the LiDia-SEQ™ system a user-friendly direct-from-specimen platform that performs genomic analysis on a microchip to provide comprehensive actionable information to clinicians in a matter of hours versus days. DNAe's initial focus is on infectious disease diagnostics starting with a groundbreaking test for bloodstream infections (BSI) and antimicrobial resistance (AMR) which uses whole blood specimens to directly detect and identify infections that lead to sepsis. This will provide clinicians with actionable information to help select the appropriate antibiotics to treat the disease. A pipeline of follow-on tests is in development for viruses and cancer testing and monitoring. DNAe has received "Breakthrough Device" designation from the US Food and Drug Administration (FDA) for its pioneering platform and first assay.

Headquartered in London UK DNAe has expanded operations further in with a clinical diagnostic development and manufacturing site in San Diego CA. We are always on the lookout for exceptional talent. For more details visit www.dnae.com.

company_description:
Johns Hopkins Medicine is a governing structure for the University's School of Medicine and the health system coordinating their research teaching patient care and related enterprises. 

The Johns Hopkins Hospital opened in 1889 followed four years later by the university's School of Medicine revolutionizing medical practice teaching and research in the United States. The hospital is now part of the Johns Hopkins Health System which includes two other acute-care hospitals and additional integrated health-care delivery components with a network of primary and specialty care practices throughout Maryland outpatient care long-term care and home care.

The Johns Hopkins University opened in 1876 as America's first research university founded for the express purpose of expanding knowledge and putting that knowledge to work for the good of humanity.

about:
My academic and research background have provided me with an excellent array of skills with which I can meaningfully contribute to the field of neuroscience research. I trained at Arizona State University (ASU) under the mentorship of Dr. Heather Bimonte-Nelson an expert in rodent learning and memory assessment. In her laboratory I studied factors associated with hormone-based therapies that influence the trajectory of cognitive and brain aging. To expand my knowledge of gender medicine and aging in the context of brain disease states I completed a postdoctoral fellowship at West Virginia University (WVU) under the mentorship of Dr. James Simpkins a well-recognized field leader in the area of neuroendocrinology and neurobiology of aging. In this laboratory I studied factors that influence stroke and post-stroke functional recovery. As part of my recruitment in 2014 Dr. Simpkins and I founded the WVU RBC a shared research instrumentation facility dedicated to the accurate efficient and ethical conduct of rodent behavioral assessments. Under my leadership the WVU RBC has grown substantially actively supporting 35+ distinct behavioral tests ~65 WVU and external scientists and 25+ manuscripts. In July of 2017 I was promoted to Research Assistant Professor. I have recently moved to Tulane University where as a new faculty I am initiating my scientific program addressing immune influence of brain function neurological injury and behavior securing research funding and developing my professional portfolio. 

Another important issue I am enthusiastic about is outreach to the public to promote science literacy understanding of neuroscience topics and support for scientific research. As the advisor for the WVU Undergraduate Neuroscience Club I oversaw their outreach efforts to local schools and community organizations including the Feed Our Brains program. Our efforts have impacted more than 5000 West Virginia children teachers parents and community members. Now that I am Tulane University I am launching a new initiative the NOLA Books & Brains program in partnership with the NOLA Public Library system.

WHAT IS DEI (DIVERSITY EQUITY INCLUSION)?
Diversity is a demographic mix of personal characteristics making each person unique. Examples of diversity include (but are not limited to) race colour ethnicity nationality gender sexual orientation disability medical condition age class and religious political or cultural beliefs.
Equity is the systematic fair and just treatment of people resulting in opportunities and outcomes for every individual that “create a level playing field”. Addressing inequities requires an understanding of the underlying causes of outcome disparities within the society and differentials in power and authority.

current_position_description:
Current R&D focus: I specialize in crafting advanced plant genotyping assays that lead the field of genetic analysis. My expertise lies in harnessing state-of-the-art ddPCR (droplet digital PCR) technology for precise detection of copy number variations (CNV) and zygosity. These assays are meticulously designed to deliver high-resolution data critical for elucidating genetic variations and their impacts.

In addition to ddPCR I employ high-throughput editing analysis to optimize and accelerate genetic modifications supporting scalable and comprehensive research across extensive sample sets. Additionally my expertise includes transgene expression profiling which assesses gene expression levels to evaluate the effectiveness of genetic modifications and their functional impact on plant systems.

Overall my work seamlessly integrates these state-of-the-art technologies to deliver pioneering and dependable solutions for plant genetic research facilitating significant improvements in ornamental traits in parent plants and the introduction of novel ornamental traits.

With an endless supply of curiosity and love for learning I gravitated toward the sciences quite naturally. I excelled in every subject in school but even more so in Biology and Chemistry. I found myself helping my fellow classmates with the coursework for those classes which only solidified my understanding of the material. As I approached applying to colleges I discovered a deep passion for the health sciences. I graduated high school with the intent to become a pharmacist but as I went through the coursework it just did not fit what I ultimately wanted my career to be. With help from my college advisor we came upon Medical Technology and everything clicked. I started taking Anatomy/Physiology and Microbiology courses and enjoyed every minute of it. I ended up graduating from the University of Montana with a Bachelor’s of Science in Medical Technology which led me to working in a lab helping physicians diagnose patients.
 I am currently a Medical Lab Scientist at Indiana University Health. In my experience here I have gained tremendous proficiency in a myriad of Serology tests from manual lateral flow and fluorescent immunoassays to automated tests performed on the MiniVidas and Bioplex. All of these skills have only been sharpened with the opportunity to teach aspiring Medical Lab Scientists as they went through their clinical year. Indiana University Health and Indiana University have teamed together to form one of the best Medical Laboratory Science programs and that is in large part due to having excellent Lab Scientists instructing students and giving them a hands-on lab experience. I feel very proud to have graduated from and still be a part of that program.

about:
My Research Interests:

The types of impoundments that beavers (Castor canadensis) create have the potential to offset some of the anticipated hydrologic and temperature changes resulting from climate change. Beaver impoundments have been shown to attenuate peak flows recharge groundwater and hyporheic flows and regulate stream temperature and base flow. Little research however has been done to quantify these benefits. I am interested in exploring how beaver dam networks modify measures of stream water quantity such as residence time base flow and hyporheic lateral flow as well as stream temperature. My research explores how effective beaver reintroductions and beaver impoundments can be at mitigating hydrologic alteration due to climate change. I'm also interested in evaluating how disturbances such as land use conversion and climate change drive species composition and life history strategies in riparian and wetland systems. Changes in flow quantity timing and water quality are likely to drive these dynamics creating future stable states in communities that are difficult to anticipate and manage. I am interested in identifying these types of changes to identify strategies to bolster resilience and inform management and policy decisions.

about:
My primary professorial appointment is in the Harvard-MIT Division of Health Sciences and Technology at Harvard Medical School where I am also a professor in the Department of Microbiology and Immunobiology. My laboratory is located at MIT where I am the Hermann von Helmholtz Professor of Health Sciences and Technology. The Harvard-MIT Program in Health Sciences and Technology was recently reorganized at MIT to form the Institute for Medical Engineering and Science (IMES). 

My laboratory studies viruses that use RNA as their genetic material (RNA viruses). We are especially interested in the group of viruses that includes dengue fever virus and West Nile virus. We want to learn more about how human cells detect viruses that invade them (innate immune responses) and how the genetic information in RNA viruses is converted to protein (translational control of gene regulation). We also work with engineers to build rapid diagnostic devices to detect dangerous viruses in the field. 

My teaching is primarily at Harvard Medical School where I teach Virology to graduate students

Over the past 9 years I have built myself up to become a scientist in research and development at OraSure Technologies with experience in the lateral flow medical device industry. 
When I started at OraSure in 2013 I had just recently graduated from East Stroudsburg University and was ready to enter the world of professionals to put my degree to use. Initially I was a manufacturing technician where I learned the ins and outs of cGMP in a clean room manufacturing setting. During this time I became proficient at device building and machine usage and helped to train many other new technicians. 
I then earned a promotion to become a Formulations Specialist. In this position I spent 7 years becoming an expert on formulations processes. Following BPRs that require use of pipettes spectrophotometers balances zatasizers reactors AKTA systems and other scientific equipment. All while checking for quality during the processes. I also had plenty of opportunities to rewrite and update work instructions batch records and standard operation procedure documents. As processes or equipment changed updated or scaled up I assisted with tech transfers validations and worked cross functionally with R&D to preform studies and get processes ready to transfer to formulations. I was a lead formulations specialist for tech transfer when OraSure developed the Ebola lateral flow device and the COVID19 lateral flow device. Then trained other formulations specialist in all integral processes. 
With my experience from manufacturing and Formulations I then secured a position in Research and Development as a Scientist 1. I have been in this position for over a year and have enjoyed helping with failure investigations processes improvement studies experimentation for developing new lateral flow devices and much more. 
Before I graduated from college I worked at a convenience store for six years in a fast-paced setting where I gain a significant number of skills interacting with the public and providing customer service. My promotion to Key Opener gave me insight into management practices. I also worked at Phillips Pet Supply where I provided customer service and sold pet products of all types. 
In my free time I enjoy spending time in nature and taking care of many animals on my small hobby farm. 
With the knowledge I have gained both in my professional and personal life I have become a well-rounded individual that looks forward to gaining more experience with an open mind.

company_description:
DCN Dx specializes in the contract development of rapid point of care tests. We offer a variety of services and products that assist our clients in the design and development of user-centric high performing diagnostic devices. 

Contract development of rapid immunoassays is at the core of all that we do at DCN Dx. We focus on lateral flow flow through and microfuidic formats. Our staff has unparalleled experience in developing assays with tough specifications regarding high sensitivity quantification multiplexing and reader integration. Our ISO 9001:2015 compliant development process allows for close integration of assay and device development pathways which ensures the best chance of commercial success for the product. 

As well as developing the assay itself DCN Dx's integrated engineering team will design develop and integrate your entire device. The engineers work closely with our scientists to create cassettes and sample handling devices that enhance the performance of your assay and meet all end-user specifications. We deliver the product not just the parts. 

DCN Dx also offers a variety of products and services to assist our clients at any stage of development including:

 - Custom private education and training courses in lateral flow technology during which we teach the details of how to develop and manufacture rapid assays. 
 - Consulting services to assist with any aspect of rapid assay development manufacturing regulation and commercialization. 
 - Custom protein-protein protein-particle small molecule-protein and small molecule- particle conjugations. 
 - Lateral flow material starter kits conjugation kits reagents cassettes and materials for use during rapid assay development. 

DCN Dx represents a single source that can help you manage and deliver completely validated assay platforms.

company_description:
Dicerna a wholly owned subsidiary of Novo Nordisk is focused on driving innovation in RNAi (ribonucleic acid interference) to selectively target and silence genes that cause or contribute to disease. Using our proprietary GalXC™ and GalXC-Plus™ RNAi technologies we discover and develop innovative RNAi-based therapies with the potential to treat both rare and more prevalent diseases. By silencing disease-causing genes our GalXC platform has the potential to address conditions that are difficult to treat with other modalities. Initially focused on disease-causing genes in the liver we have continued to innovate and are exploring new applications of our RNAi technology with GalXC-Plus which expands on the functionality and application of our flagship liver-targeted GalXC technology to tissues and cell types outside the liver with the potential to treat diseases across multiple therapeutic areas. 

Our team brings together talented experts across the fields of biology chemistry clinical science medicine and more. With decades of scientific and technical experience focused on RNAi technology we have the knowledge and experience needed to discover and develop therapies for patients with serious unmet medical needs. Between our own pipeline of core discovery and clinical candidates and those developed through collaborative relationships with some of the world's leading pharmaceutical companies we have generated more than 20 active discovery preclinical or clinical proprietary and collaborative programs focused on rare cardiometabolic viral chronic liver and complement-mediated diseases as well as neurodegenerative diseases and pain.

I have been working at the University of Wyoming as a senior PhD student since 2018 for the following projects:
-Development of a rapid highly sensitive handheld Raman-based device for detection of SARS-CoV-2 (the virus that causes COVID-19) and serological responses against SARS-CoV-2.

-Genetically engineering of a magnetotactic bacterium to express West Nile virus antigen on naturally produced bacterial magnetic nanoparticles to be used in immunoassays.

Community Guidelines:

1. We want to foster positive conversation around the issues we are passionate about. To that end we remove profanity content that contains threatening language content that is aimed at private individuals personal information and repeated unwanted messages.

2. Don't mention any medicines by name — ours or anyone else's.
Because of the fair balance rules governing our industry we cannot post any comments that reference any pharmaceutical brand product or service. Please do not mention any specific medicines by name or include any links to third party sites in your comments.

3. This isn't the place to report or discuss side effects.
This site is not intended as a forum for reporting side effects experienced while taking a Genentech product. Instead you should report any side effects to Genentech Drug Safety at 1-888-835-2555. You can also report side effects of any prescription product directly to the FDA at 1-800-FDA-1088 or by visiting www.FDA.gov/medwatch.

4. Don't pitch your product or service.
Please don't use our page as a place to promote your product or pitch your services. Please also avoid posting links to external sites. We reserve the right to remove any posts that are deemed promotional.

company_description:
KNH has turned 112 years and it had its Centenary Celebration in 2001. The Hospital was built to fulfill the role of being a National Referral and Teaching Hospital as well as to provide medical research environment.

Established in 1901 with a bed capacity of 40 KNH became a State Corporation in 1987 with a Board of Management and is at the apex of the referral system in the Health Sector in Kenya. It covers an area of 45.7 hectares and within the KNH complex are College of Health Sciences (University of Nairobi) the Kenya Medical Training College Kenya Medical Research Institute and National Laboratory Service (Ministry of Health).

KNH has 50 wards 22out-patient clinics 24 theaters (16 specialized) and Accident & Emergency Department. Out of the total bed capacity of 1800 209 beds are for the Private Wing.

Kenyatta National Hospital in Nairobi is the oldest hospital in Kenya. Founded in 1901 with a bed capacity of 40 as the Native Civil hospital it was renamed the King George VI in 1952. At that time the settler community were served by the nearby European Hospital (now Nairobi Hospital). It was renamed Kenyatta National Hospital — after Jomo Kenyatta — following independence from the British. It is currently the largest referral and teaching hospital in the country.

Kenyatta National Hospital has a capacity of 1800 beds and has over 6000 staff members. It covers an area of 45.7 hectares. The University of Nairobi Medical School and several government agencies are located on the campus.

company_description:
Exosome Diagnostics Inc. a subsidiary of mdxhealth is commercializing minimally invasive molecular diagnostics in blood and urine to enable doctors to select optimal therapies for cancer and other diseases. 
 
Exosomes are lipid bilayer covered microvesicles shed by solid tumors into body fluids such as blood and urine. Exosomes contain nucleic acid profiles of cancers and other diseases. In published studies Exosome Diagnostics has demonstrated the ability to extract exosomes from the blood and urine of patients and to detect with high sensitivity and specificity key genes and gene mutations associated with a variety of cancers.

Roger Peck is an experienced research scientist and portfolio manager with extensive background leading studies involving product development of diagnostics suitable for low-resource settings. Mr. Peck led PATH's work assessing HIV rapid test quality and HIV self-test technologies that resulted in the first publicly available Target Product Profile for HIV self-tests. Currently he leads PATH’s activities with assessing the quality of rapid and laboratory-based HIV assays and reagents for the USAID PEPFAR Global Health Supply Chain Quality Assurance project manages the Washington COVID-19 Biorepository and leads PATH’s work in developing rapid tests for surveillance of primary immunodeficiency disease and novel HIV self-tests. As Associate Director of Technology Advancement at PATH he is responsible for leading and implementing PATH's strategy for product development and evaluation. Additionally he is responsible for project management laboratory-based tasks quality assurance and other activities related to the development of diagnostic tests for neglected tropical diseases malaria and other infectious diseases and conditions. Previously he led PATH’s work in developing and implementing rapid tests for onchocerciasis and lymphatic filariasis and the quality systems necessary to support the tests as well as leading PATH’s supported product development within the cervical cancer diagnostic technologies (START) for both HPV nucleic acid and onchoprotein tests. Before joining PATH he worked at OraSure Technologies where he conducted product design and development. He also oversaw several aspects of OraQuick HIV production scale-up including complex automated equipment acquisition installation and training. company_description:
Gravidas is developing a rapid PoC test to identify pregnant women at risk of developing severe or life-threatening preeclampsia. 

Founded and managed by practicing clinical and scientific leaders in assay development and pregnancy and preeclampsia Gravidas is at an advanced stage of product development.

There is a significant unmet medical for a simple test for determining who is at risk for developing severe preeclampsia due to the current low-confidence diagnosis of pregnant women presenting with preeclamptic symptoms. Without such a test there may be mistimed or unnecessary medical intervention leading to rapid progression to severe disease and pregnancy/delivery complications.

Preeclampsia is the leading cause of maternal and fetal mortality and morbidity worldwide resulting in a large addressable market in the U.S. and around the world for such a test.

about: Jeff has been working in the diagnostics industry for 40 years. He began working as a Medical Technologist in 1983 for Animal Disease Division of the Oregon Department of Agriculture. There he participated in the development of novel ELISA technology to detect pathogens in livestock. In 1987 Jeff became a member of the microbiology development team at Syva Company in Palo Alto CA developing immunoassays against human pathogens. His contributions enabled the successful launch of two FDA approved EIA's for Chlamydia and Hepatitis A. Upon joining Epitope Inc. in 1992 Jeff became a senior member of the R & D staff and for 5 years participated in the development of a number of new and unique assay technologies primarily focusing on the detection of analytes in saliva. In 1997 he joined AFem Medical Corp. in Portland OR. Jeff and his staff of 5 scientists performed contract R & D in the field of rapid lateral-flow tests and additionally developed a patented rapid assay for a nicotine metabolite in saliva. BioDot recognizing Jeff's experience and knowledge base in the diagnostics industry recruited him to help lead their revolutionary One Step... One Solution technology program. Jeff was a founder and VP of Technology for Diagnostic Consulting Network (DCN Diagnostics) in 2003 prior to founding Kestrel BioSciences in June of 2008. Kestrel BioSciences is a leader in lateral-flow assay and Point-of-Care technologies for low resource environments with R & D and production facilities in Bangkok Thailand and Carlsbad CA USA. In 2017 Kestrel BioSciences USA was acquired by Planet Innovation Pty Ltd. and re-branded Lumos Diagnostics where Jeff served as the VP of Business & Product Development.

company_description: VitaScan is a point-of-care diagnostics company that develops simple scalable and quantitative tests to solve big problems. Our first product IronScan rapidly identifies iron deficiency a health condition that affects one-third of the world population. Each year iron deficiency causes over 100 000 maternal deaths and raises risk of miscarriage and stillbirth for 500 million women. Another 500 million young children face permanent development problems through their critical early years. We aim to make IronScan the new standard of care and enable on-the-spot test and treatment for all who suffer from iron deficiency.

company_description:
Who are we?

ASML is an innovation leader in the global semiconductor industry. We make machines that chipmakers use to mass produce microchips. Founded in 1984 in the Netherlands with just a handful of employees we've now grown to over 40 000 employees 143 nationalities and more than 60 locations around the world.

What do we do?

We provide chipmakers with hardware software and services to mass produce patterns on silicon through lithography. Our lithography systems use ultraviolet light to create billions of tiny structures on silicon that together make up a microchip. We push our technology to new limits to enable our customers to create smaller faster and more powerful chips.

Who are our people?

While you may think that only engineers and mathematicians work at ASML you'll be surprised to find out that our people come from a wide variety of backgrounds. Across ASML we have dedicated teams that manage customer support communications and media IT software development and more. Every team in the company is essential for pushing our technology and the industry forward.

If you love to tackle challenges and innovate in a collaborative supportive and inclusive environment with all the flexibility and freedom to unleash your full potential ASML is the place to be.

Join us!

about:
I have been working in research and product development to improve healthcare and diagnostics. Particularly my interest is in molecular diagnostics medical devices data science and clinical research to map human health. I am also driven by technologies that bridge the gap between developed countries and low-resource settings.

I am currently engaged in data analysis research manuscript and software development within the realm of immunology and immunoassays focusing on target proteins including cytokines at the Human Immune Monitoring Center.

Previously I’ve worked to develop and evaluate rapid diagnostic tests for infectious diseases including COVID-19. I also built machine learning pipelines for image classification as a tool for interpretation of rapid test results. 

Prior to joining my previous companies I was a PhD student in bioengineering to develop highly sensitive diagnostic tools for point-of-care testing to detect infectious disease targets such as influenza and Ebola viruses.

I collaborated with nonprofit organization company and hospital for product prototyping and clinical trial. I am excited about interdisciplinary works with people from different backgrounds.

Before life as PhD student I was a product developer in a medical device company. I have focused on 1) developing balloon catheters for cardiovascular treatment to launch in the US market 2) working on material selection device fabrication and functional assessment and 3) analyzing ~30 patents to avoid patent infringement in the US.

Besides my work as a scientist I organized a networking group for mainly (but not limited to) Japanese people in Seattle to provide opportunities to share their experiences in both Japan and the US.

Recent accomplishments:
• Developed COVID-19 rapid antigen test within ~6 months by both assay development and transferring to manufacturing group
• Received EUA approval for COVID-19 rapid antigen test within ~6 months by conducting both in-house studies and on-site clinical trial
• Developed a tool for interpretation of rapid test result by both ML model and signal quantification

Skill:
Molecular diagnostics (using blood urine nasal swab) product and assay development immunoassay nucleic acid amplification test (NAAT) fluorescence imaging Python machine learning pipeline (TensorFlow Kubeflow Elyra Docker) image classification reinforcement learning (Stable Baselines OpenAI) data analysis (signal quantification logistic/sigmoid function ROC/AUC statistical distance of probability distributions F1-score)

company_description:
Sana Biotechnology is focused on utilizing engineered cells as medicines for patients. The ability to modify genes and use cells as medicines will be one of the most important advances in healthcare over the next several decades. Sana is building differentiated capabilities across the spectrum of cell and gene therapy. 
 
Three aspirations drive Sana as we look to discover treatments for patients with poor outcomes or currently untreatable diseases. The first is the ability to repair and control the genes in any cell in the body. We are advancing novel delivery technologies with the goal of being able to deliver any payload to any cell in a specific predictable and repeatable manner paving the way for next-generation in vivo gene therapy. Next is the ability to differentiate pluripotent stem cells ex vivo into immune-cloaked functional cells with the aspiration of being able to replace any missing or damaged cells in the body. Last is a belief we can enable broader access to our therapies through focusing on scalable manufacturing solutions the cost of manufacturing and aligning with key stakeholders.
 
Sana launched in early 2019 and has over 350 employees in Seattle Cambridge and South San Francisco. Sana was founded with the long view – bringing together great people and the best technologies to deliver on the challenging promise of using engineered cells to meaningfully change the outcome of many human diseases.

Comments on a post that do not relate to the subject matter of the original post

Comments advocating illegal activity
Posts violating copyrights or trademarks
Advertisement or promotion of commercial products services entities or individuals
Duplicative comments by the same user or multiple users. In the case of identical comments only the first submission will be approved.

company_description:
GattaCo is an medical diagnostics company that has developed a revolutionary new technology that will to bring clinical-quality IVD data to Telemedicine Digital Health and mHealth settings.

Most medical diagnostics that test for things in blood cannot actually work with whole blood directly but require the blood to be spun in a centrifuge to obtain plasma then a precise volume of plasma is measured and added to a diagnostic kit. GattaCo has developed the first easy to use disposable fully automatic and rapid centrifuge replacement tool the Sípon. The Sípon eliminates the current dependence on phlebotomists vacutainers centrifuges and lab techs needed for performing even simple blood-based diagnostic testing. With the Sípon or its technology integrated into a diagnostic product lab-quality diagnostic data will be available anywhere it is needed.

company_description:
Drugs & Diagnostics for Tropical Diseases (DDTD) is a unique non-profit (501c3) venture based in San Diego CA with a mission "to discover new treatments and diagnostics for neglected tropical diseases". These diseases have been typically neglected from research and development efforts due to the perceived lack of profitability and are commonly referred to as Neglected Tropical Diseases or NTDs.

As part of our mission we are developing easy to use rapid inexpensive diagnostics to specifically detect infections associated with loiasis lymphatic filariasis and river blindness. We are passionate about serving the needs of the underprivileged within our capacity as scientists. In addition we believe it is important to increase public awareness of the dire need to improve global health. The community's resulting civic engagement can make an impact globally and DDTD wants to lead these efforts.

At an international level DDTD embraces the idea of eliminating diseases that do not belong in the 21st century. The first step towards eliminating a disease is the ability to detect it. Mapping diseases is critical for the World Health Organization (WHO) and its affiliates to initiate mass drug administration programs allocate resources monitor progress and eventually verify elimination. DDTD focuses on Neglected Tropical Diseases (NTDs) a group of diseases that afflicts 1 in 6 people or approximately 1.4 billion individuals worldwide. NTDs are severely debilitating lower cognitive function in children and hamper economic development. Worldwide 149 countries and territories are affected by at least one NTD resulting in approximately 57 million years of life lost due to premature disability.

At a domestic level we seek to reduce health inequity within local communities by providing a portfolio of low-cost rapid diagnostic tests (RDTs) developed specifically for homeless individuals and people living in precarious conditions.

about: Dr. Qi Xiao is a highly skilled researcher with extensive experience in organic chemistry biotechnology and biomedical engineering. D. in 2014 from the University of Tokyo Japan under the guidance of Prof. Takuzo Aida focusing on liquid crystalline semiconducting materials with fused oligothiophenes helical and cyclic oligothiophenes and their helicity and aromaticity. Dr. Xiao's academic journey continued with postdoctoral research positions at Temple University under the supervision of Prof. Michael L Klein and the University of Pennsylvania under the supervision of Prof. Virgil Percec jointly where he investigated dendrimer assemblies polymerization of aramids biomimetic membrane systems and lipid nanoparticles as a delivery platform for mRNA delivery in collaboration with Nobel laureate Prof. Drew Weissman. From 2022 to 2024 Dr. Xiao worked in Gator Bio/ET Healthcare Inc contributing to the development of bio-layer Interferometry (BLI) biosensors for antibody AAV and DNA assays and optimization of immunoassays.

about: I am a scientist with decades of academic experience and hundreds of presentations in my country and internationally. My basic research has accumulated knowledge about the biology on the number one non-viral curable sexually transmitted infection caused by Trichomonas vaginalis (Tv). This basic research on this organism contributed to our understanding of the molecular basis of pathogenesis. Over 42 years of studying this organism led to the identification of virulence factors that permit for infections in both women and men. More recently is the development and invention of patentable Point-of-Care diagnostics for women (OSOM Tv test). I then developed an approach to create chimeric epitope chain proteins will lead to the synthesis of highly specific and unique targets for the development of serodiagnostic tests for infectious diseases using T. vaginalis as a model (Int'l J Environ Res Pub Hlth. 2020. 17:5783. PMID 32785073). More recently I invented the POC prick-test serodiagnostic called SentriTv for detection of antibody to Tv from a drop of blood (Pathogens 12(1):77 Pearl Antigenics LLC pearlantigenics.com). This simple test fullfills the WHO ASSURED criteria of being Affordable Sensitive Specific User-friendly Rapid/Robust Equipment-free and Deliverable to low-resource areas worldwide.
